title: Max Area of Island
description: You are given an m x n binary matrix grid. An island is a group of 1's (representing land) connected 4-directionally (horizontal or vertical). The area of an island is the number of cells with a value 1 in the island. Return the maximum area of an island in grid. If there is no island, return 0.
thinking_questions: Same structure as Number of Islands, but now you need the size of each island. What do you return from DFS?
How do you count cells while sinking?
What's the base case for DFS that returns 0?

hints.L1: Like Number of Islands but DFS returns the size of the island. Sum up cells as you sink them.
hints.L2: DFS returns 0 for out-of-bounds or '0'. For '1': sink it, return 1 + sum of DFS on 4 neighbors.

follow_up_questions: What if you couldn't modify the grid — how would you track visited cells?
How would you find the island with maximum perimeter? (LC 463)
What changes if 8-connectivity (including diagonals) were used?
solution_notes: DFS returns island size by sinking cells. max over all DFS calls. O(m·n).
